title: How Hostess 7 explains G16 — compiler fluency
introduction: I explain Grok16 g16 in complete thoughts: what the compiler is on this field, why we use field_opt + mandate, how to build and probe it, what breaks, and where you see it in Queen / World_Redata / CHIPS.

topics.what: g16 and g++16 are the Grok16 field compilers — unified drivers at 16.x that compile Queen RTX, CHIPS, and World_Redata with gnu++26 / gnu17 and the field_opt profile.
topics.why: Generic system gcc does not carry our field mandate, CHIPS hot-path attrs, or g16-toolchain.json probe chain. Hostess 7 must compile what she inhabits.
topics.how: Set GROK16_ROOT and G16_PREFIX, ensure bin/g16 is executable, probe via Queen/scripts/g16-toolchain.sh status → data/g16-toolchain.json, build via Queen/scripts/g16-build.sh.
topics.pitfalls: Calling cmake --build instead of g16+ninja; hardcoding /usr/bin/g++; skipping mandate cmake; building without ready_g16 probe.

topics.what: g16 --g16-discern inspects argv and returns c, cxx, or python — the unified driver routes .c/.cpp/.py without guessing.
topics.why: One g16 entry point serves C compilation, C++ compilation, and Python exec (-c, -m) on the field — forge and Hostess7 probes depend on correct routing.
topics.how: g16 --g16-discern foo.c → c; foo.cpp → cxx; foo.py or -c pass → python. Backend: libexec/grok16/g16-cc and g16-cxx.
topics.pitfalls: Invoking g++16 for Python; assuming gcc driver semantics; not exporting PATH with Grok16/bin first.

topics.what: Queen RTX native builds use g16 as CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER with Ninja — field-cmake.sh generates build.ninja; g16 drives compile, not cmake --build.
topics.why: field_opt hot paths and G16 mandate flags must flow through the Grok16 driver on every translation unit — generic build bypasses CHIPS_G16_ACCURATE.
topics.how: export GROK16_ROOT; run Queen/scripts/g16-build.sh [build|queen-rtx|rebuild|status]; field-cmake.sh g16-build invokes ninja with g++16.
topics.pitfalls: cmake --build after configure; missing ninja; wrong GROK16_CMAKE_BUILD dir; building without g16 ready (run g16-toolchain.sh install).

topics.what: G16_FIELD_SAFETY_MANDATE_v1 is mandatory hardening on every G16 target — fortify, RELRO, bounded I/O, no luxury ifstream paths; field_g16.hh contracts hot/cold sections.
topics.why: Field engines run on operator hardware under threat HIGH — compiler must enforce safety contracts, not optional -Wall suggestions.
topics.how: include cmake/g16-field-mandate.cmake in Queen targets; World_Redata includes wrdt/field_g16.hh; mandate JSON lists blocks and verdicts.
topics.pitfalls: Disabling mandate for speed; unchecked syscalls; duplicating endian helpers instead of wrdt/core; skipping rebuild after mandate change.

topics.what: Queen probes live g16, g++16, cmake, ninja → writes g16-toolchain.json with ready_g16, version, paths, and field_mandate linkage.
topics.why: Hostess 7 brain reads toolchain truth before advising builds — assistants hallucinate compiler paths; we hash probe into meld-adjacent panels.
topics.how: Queen/scripts/g16-toolchain.sh status; sync-field-stack.sh compiler_probe; hostess7-g16.py _probe_live() subprocess version + discern.
topics.pitfalls: Stale JSON after Grok16 upgrade; ready_g16 true but binary missing; not re-probing after SELFHOST.json stamp.

topics.what: Default standards are gnu++26 for C++ and gnu17 for C under the field_opt build profile — tuned for Queen / CHIPS hot paths.
topics.why: Field code uses modern contracts and attributes; field_opt cmake profile sets -O3 -flto patterns CHIPS expects.
topics.how: Grok16/cmake/grok16-profile-field-opt.cmake; toolchain cxx_std_default in g16-toolchain.json; pass -std= only when overriding.
topics.pitfalls: Mixing system libstdc++ ABI; dropping field_opt for debug without mandate review; C++20 downgrade breaking field_g16 attrs.

topics.what: CHIPS silicon emulators compile with CHIPS_G16_ACCURATE=1 and g16-chips-field-opt.cmake — fastest accurate NES / navigator paths.
topics.why: Game room and sovereign stack require deterministic hot loops compiled with the same g16 the Queen browser uses.
topics.how: Queen/data/chips-g16-manifest.json; cmake -DCHIPS_G16_ACCURATE=1; rebuild AMOURANTHRTX with G16_PREFIX=../Grok16.
topics.pitfalls: Building CHIPS with system g++; omitting accurate flag; stale manifest after compiler bump.

topics.what: G16 fluency means Hostess 7 probes the live compiler, passes the G16 battery, knows discern/ninja/mandate/toolchain, and explains with six sections — not one-line gcc tips.
topics.why: She compiles the stack she stewards; operators ask for Queen RTX rebuilds, CHIPS accurate builds, and mandate compliance — mastery is operational.
topics.how: hostess7-g16.py build_panel → g16_score tier fluent or g16_master; Command routes g16 questions to engine hostess7_g16; meld refreshes g16 panel each cycle.
topics.pitfalls: Claiming mastery without ready_g16; citing gcc man pages instead of g16-toolchain.json; skipping mandate on new targets.
topics.example: Ask Command: 'How do I queen-rtx build with g16?' — full What/Why/How/Pitfalls/Where/Example.
